title: Ferrofluid Field
description: A puddle of magnetic fluid under a field. As the field rises the surface goes unstable — the Rosensweig normal-field instability — and erupts into a hexagonal array of peaks along the field lines. It's a real height field: a connected pool with peaks whose height tracks the local field, shaded per pixel like the oily black chrome it is — diffuse, tight specular, a reflection-vector environment off the surface normal, and Fresnel edge sheen — so peaks glint at the tips and catch reflected bands on their flanks over mirror-dark valleys. Move the mouse: it's a magnet that pulls the peaks up. Field strength, spike density, magnet pull, wobble, and sheen hue are live and audio-mappable (beats surge the field).
